mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% from a lower level, it signals ongoing labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.5% and is rising, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both housing starts and retail sales signals a broad demand contraction that feeds into lower corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when both HOUST and RSAFS are declining month-over-month, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: After a sharp drop in industrial production, a subsequent stabilization or recovery in INDPRO signals that supply-side disruptions are subsiding, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > -0.5% after having been < -1.0%
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO stabilizes after a decline, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A steady increase in the Consumer Price Index above 200 signals persistent inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise interest rates, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive more often than not when CPIAUCSL is above 200 and rising, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,000,000 units (annualized), it indicates severe weakness in the housing market that drags on economic growth and financial sector health, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts fell below 1M in April 2008
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of quarters when HOUST is below 1000, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with low federal funds rate indicates a liquidity trap where monetary policy loses effectiveness, amplifying pessimism about economic recovery and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5% and FEDFUNDS < 3%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: After a period of aggressive monetary easing (FEDFUNDS below 2%), a stabilization or increase in retail sales indicates that demand is responding to stimulus, boosting corporate earnings expectations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary stimulus with demand recovery
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS <= 2% and RSAFS > 380000, the hypothesis is rejected.
